parse.precondition: Reader supplying no CSS tokens (stubbed JsonTokenIterator.hasNext to return false immediately)
parse.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() called once; then CssContentHandler.endDocument() called once; no further handler invocations
pseudocode: verify docHandler.startDocument() once; verify docHandler.endDocument() once;

parse.description: Single ATKEYWORD token: take branch where type==ATKEYWORD and handleRuleSet invoked then loop ends
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns iterator with one CssToken of type ATKEYWORD, then hasNext returns false after; stub handleRuleSet to record invocation
parse.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() called; handleRuleSet invoked once; CssContentHandler.endDocument() called; no exception
pseudocode: assert handleRuleSet called once; docHandler.endDocument() called once

parse.description: Single non-ATKEYWORD token: take branch where type!=ATKEYWORD and handleAtRule invoked then loop ends
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns iterator with one CssToken of type IDENT (non-ATKEYWORD); stub handleAtRule to record invocation
parse.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() called; handleAtRule invoked once; CssContentHandler.endDocument() called; no exception
pseudocode: assert handleAtRule called once; docHandler.endDocument() called once

parse.description: Multiple tokens >1 iterations: alternating types to cover two-loop iterations
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns iterator with two tokens [ATKEYWORD, IDENT], then empty
parse.expectedResult: handleRuleSet called once; handleAtRule called once; start and endDocument each once; no exception
pseudocode: assert handleRuleSet and handleAtRule each called once; docHandler.endDocument() once

parse.description: PrematureEOFException thrown inside handleAtRule causes loop break and normal endDocument
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns one non-ATKEYWORD token; stub handleAtRule to throw PrematureEOFException
parse.expectedResult: handleAtRule throws PrematureEOFException; parser catches and breaks; docHandler.endDocument() called once; no rethrow
pseudocode: assert no exception escapes; docHandler.endDocument() once

parse.description: IOException thrown by scan(reader,...) propagates out of parse
parse.precondition: Stub scan() to throw IOException("io error")
parse.expectedResult: IOException with message "io error" is thrown; no handler methods invoked
pseudocode: Reader reader = any; stub scan() to throw IOException("io error");

parse.description: CssException thrown by scan(reader,...) propagates out of parse
parse.precondition: Stub scan() to throw CssException(new CssGrammarException(...))
parse.expectedResult: CssException is thrown; no startDocument or endDocument calls
pseudocode: Reader reader = any; stub scan() to throw CssException;

parse.description: Single token then second iteration yields hasNext false: verify correct exit B7â†’B1â†’B8
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns one ATKEYWORD token; then hasNext returns false
parse.expectedResult: handleRuleSet invoked and endDocument invoked; no extra calls
pseudocode: verify handleRuleSet() once; verify endDocument() once

parse.description: Mixed tokens >2 iterations to exercise deeper loop sequence
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns [IDENT, ATKEYWORD], then empty
parse.expectedResult: handleAtRule and handleRuleSet each called once; start/endDocument once
pseudocode: assert both handlers called; endDocument once

parse.description: PrematureEOFException thrown in handleRuleSet is caught and break from loop
parse.precondition: Stub scan() returns one ATKEYWORD token; stub handleRuleSet to throw PrematureEOFException
parse.expectedResult: no exception escapes; docHandler.endDocument() called once after break
pseudocode: assert no exception escapes; docHandler.endDocument() once
